text:
There once was a wise old fox named Fredrick. Fredrick lived in old oak trees hollow far from human botherations. One fine spring morning, Fredrick woke feeling odd. His eyes weren't seeing as sharp. A quick hop around his home seemed slower than usual.

That evening news tickler was peculiar. A possum who couldn't climb seemed lost. Fredrick knew. He listened attentively while the possum recounted odd tales — like a carrot tree and a cloud that moaned melancholy.

Fredrick suggested some branches might be tricky. Together they explored. There were ladders made from twigs. That possum could climb!

Thanks to Fredrick’s wisdom, the possum didn't perish. The forest didn’t gossip.

The tales they exchanged? Wildlife folklore. Fredrick felt glad. They're treasures. An old friend.

So Fredrick's life continued. He still watched the sky. Now, every now and then, he'd share a laugh. And perhaps,
